name: bridge - mountain climber (cross body)
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Engage your core and lift your right foot off the ground, bringing your right knee towards your left elbow.
Return your right foot to the starting position and repeat the movement with your left foot towards your right elbow.
Continue alternating sides, moving at a controlled pace.
Keep your hips level and avoid lifting your hips too high or sagging them too low.
Maintain a steady breathing pattern throughout the exercise.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line. Engage your core and lift your right foot off the ground, bringing your right knee towards your left elbow. Return your right foot to the starting position and repeat the movement with your left foot towards your right elbow. Continue alternating sides, moving at a controlled pace. Keep your hips level and avoid lifting your hips too high or sagging them too low. Maintain a steady breathing pattern throughout the exercise. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
